name: Marine War Risk Insurance
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **AWRP AT ~0.8-1% H&M / 7-DAY BASELINE — INSURERS NOT YET PRICING MOU FRAMEWORK AS DURABLE; CMA CGM SAN ANTONIO STRIKE MAY 5 KEEPS KINETIC-TAIL RISK LIVE.** Yesterday's reframed trigger ('AWRP normalization toward 0.5% within 10 sessions' by May 16) is on Day 1-2 with no observable normalization yet. Lloyd's List / Strauss Center: AWRP for Gulf transits remains around 0.8-1% Hull-and-Machinery value per 7-day period after no-claims bonuses — 'down from peaks around 2.5-5% but still 5-8x the pre-war 0.1-0.15% norm.' VLCC TD3C MEG-China benchmark sustained at ~$420-474K/day TCE; one peak Indian-petrochemical charter reportedly hit $770K/day during crisis-extreme phase. Hormuz transit volume 5-6 vessels/day (May 3-4) vs 138 historical norm — pause has not produced transit-volume recovery. **CMA CGM San Antonio cruise-missile strike May 5 demonstrates operational risk persists despite diplomatic pause** — proximate cause of AWRP staying elevated rather than normalizing; CBS attribution to Iran (single-source US officials, IRGC has NOT claimed). **The paper-vs-physical bifurcation IS the structural intelligence:** paper markets (Brent futures, equities) priced peace-dividend ~50-70%; physical/insurance/IRR markets priced peace-dividend ~10-20%. Joint War Committee Persian Gulf 'high-risk' designation MAINTAINED. France's Charles de Gaulle CSG redeployment Suez→Red Sea/Gulf of Aden adds new naval principal — could over time depress AWRP if European naval presence reduces transit risk perception, but operational impact is days/weeks away.

current.cover_current: $10-14M per Persian Gulf voyage; broad rates 0.8-1.5%; US/UK/Israeli flags 3x; some easing to 0.35-0.45% on broader MEG routes. 329 vessels = ~$352B coverage gap.

VLCC TD3C ~$420-474K/day TCE sustained; STEEPEST 1-DAY DROP IN 5+ YEARS ON MOU NEWS ('LEAD BALLOON' — Lloyd's List). Hormuz transits 5-6/day vs 138 norm.
